latest.title: Result of General Meeting and Issue of Equity
latest.content:
Today 10:32
RNS Number : 7144L
Ondo InsurTech PLC
09 July 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF UK DOMESTIC LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 (AS AMENDED) ("UK MAR"). UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE. THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
Terms used but not defined in this announcement have the same meaning as set out in the Company's announcement released at 4:05 pm on
22 June
2026.
9 July 2026
Ondo InsurTech Plc
("Ondo" or the "Company")
Result of General Meeting, Issue of Equity, Capital Reorganisation
and Total Voting Rights
Ondo InsurTech Plc (LSE: ONDO), a leading provider of claims prevention technology for home insurers, is pleased to announce that at the General Meeting ("
GM
") held earlier today, all resolutions, as set out in the GM notice published on 24 June 2026, were duly passed on a poll. Resolutions 1-3 were passed as ordinary resolutions and resolutions 4-6 were passed as special resolutions.
The results of the poll were as follows:
FOR
AGAINST
FOR
%
AGAINST
%
ABSTAIN

44,729,887
205,087

0.46
72,289
Completion of Fundraising and Capital Reorganisation
As a result of the resolutions being passed, the Company will allot 96,710,000 New Ordinary Shares comprising 87,039,000 Placing Shares and 9,671,000 Retail Offer Shares, subject to Admission, to raise gross proceeds of £2.9 million. The Company is also today issuing unsecured Convertible Loan Notes totalling £2.0 million.
As set out in the Circular and following the passing of the Resolutions, the Capital Reorganisation will now take effect to facilitate the issuance and Admission of the New Ordinary Shares pursuant to the Fundraising. Under the Capital Reorganisation, each Existing Ordinary Share of 5 pence nominal value will be subdivided and reclassified into one New Ordinary Share of 1 pence each and one new Deferred Share of 4 pence each.
Admission
Application has been made to the London Stock Exchange for 96,710,000 New Ordinary Shares to trading on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange. It is expected that admission will become effective and that dealings in the New Ordinary Shares will commence at 8.00 a.m. on 10 July 2026.
The New Ordinary Shares, when issued, will be fully paid and will rank
pari passu
in all respects with each other and with the existing Ordinary Shares of the Company, including, without limitation, the right to receive all dividends and other distributions declared, made or paid after the date of issue.
Total Voting Rights
Following Admission of the New Ordinary Shares, the Company's issued share capital will comprise 246,524,760 Ordinary Shares all carrying one voting right each. The Company does not hold any shares in treasury. Accordingly, the total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will be 246,524,760, which may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company pursuant to the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Enquiries
For further information, please visit www.ondoplc.com or contact the following:
Ondo InsurTech Plc
Mark Wood, Executive Chairman

company_30s.what_they_do: ONDO is a small‑cap technology firm that builds and licenses a cloud‑based platform for managing digital asset transactions. The product suite includes real‑time settlement engines, compliance modules, and an API that lets fintechs integrate token‑based payments into their existing workflows.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Digital assets are a growing sector, with institutional demand for secure, low‑cost settlement solutions rising sharply. ONDO’s platform is positioned to capture this niche by offering a modular, scalable solution that can be tailored to banks, exchanges, and fintech startups, giving it a competitive edge over legacy settlement providers.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a rebuilding phase: it has recently completed a retail place‑and‑sell, raised fresh equity, and its directors are buying shares. The business is still in the early‑stage product development cycle, relying on the capital raise to move from prototype to a market‑ready offering.

rns_narrative: ONDO has been busy raising capital through a series of placing and retail offers, indicating that the management team is actively seeking liquidity to fund development and scale.
Directors and key employees are buying shares and participating in a share incentive plan, which signals internal confidence and aligns their interests with shareholders.
The company is also engaging in board restructuring and debt restructuring, aiming to strengthen governance and reduce leverage.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism driven by fresh capital and insider buying.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter focuses on the directors’ purchases and the new equity raise as proof that smart money believes in the platform’s potential.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the heavy dilution from the placing, the lack of any current revenue, and the risk that the product may not hit the market fast enough.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The conversation has moved from skeptical to cautiously hopeful as the capital raise closes and the share incentive plan goes live.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for ONDO are flat, meaning the market has largely ignored the company – a good sign for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the latest equity placing and the release of the associated share volume reports.
Announcement of the refinancing plan that will replace the existing high‑cost debt with a longer‑term, lower‑interest facility.
First operational milestone: the platform goes live in a pilot with a mid‑size fintech partner.
A surge in trading volume that exceeds the 30‑day moving average, signaling renewed investor interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a breakout above the 30p support level with volume 20% above the 30‑day average. Any statement from the board about using the raised funds for product launch or a new partnership will also be a green flag.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a mean‑reversion cycle – the crash has been deep, but the APEX score shows a tight price range, suggesting the next move is imminent once a catalyst confirms that the bottom is reached.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The equity crash has taken the share price 70‑95% below its pre‑crash peak. If the company delivers on its product roadmap and the capital raise is used effectively, the price could rebound to about half of that peak, delivering roughly a 200% upside. Historical RED and PURPLE signals from similar setups show that the highest returns come from these deep crashes.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is dilution – the equity placing adds significant shares to the market, which could keep the price depressed if the company fails to generate revenue quickly. Additionally, the lack of current earnings means the upside relies entirely on a successful product launch, which is uncertain.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is about 2–3 times the current price, while the downside is limited to the current level because the stock has already fallen that far. The asymmetry is therefore roughly 2:1.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once the price clears 35p on a volume spike of at least 10% above the 30‑day average. Use a stop 5p below entry to protect against a false breakout. Position sizing should be conservative – 5–10% of the portfolio – because the upside is large but the volatility remains high.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see a volume surge of 20% and the price reaches 30p. That combination would signal a credible mean‑reversion move.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks identified.

ai_insights.bottom_line: ONDO sits at a deep crash with a fresh capital raise and insider confidence. If the product launch triggers a volume spike, the upside could be 2–3× the current price, making this a compelling contrarian opportunity.
